rationale: Both candidates verify the already-resolved Med (qwen3_tts gating) and Nit (seq_len removal) concerns and both surface the live Low concern (async_chunk default fallback), but Y's version is more precisely grounded — it cites the exact sibling default elsewhere in the same file and the real field default, closely mirroring the ground-truth Low comment. X's remaining findings (extract a qwen3_tts helper, add explanatory comments) are valid but low-value stylistic notes rather than substantive risks. Neither candidate addresses amy-why's design question about relocating the model-specific check. Critically, Y explicitly questions whether the split now only handles the scheduled-token axis and notes hidden-shape-aligned tensors would no longer slice — directly anticipating the dual-batch-axis issue later fixed in PR #4910 — while X's closest analog (a generic shape-mismatch assertion suggestion) never frames the issue in terms of multiple batch axes.
